page: Google Cloud Platform e2-highmem-2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance from the E2 family. It features 2 vCPUs mapped to 1 physical Intel Xeon core with 2 threads per core, running at a clock speed of 2.2 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark evaluations indicate that the e2-highmem-2 occupies a lower-tier performance bracket. In stress-ng testing, single-core CPU performance is rated as weak, while multi-core performance falls into the bottom decile. Memory bandwidth tests show read-only small block speeds of 80.53 GB/sec, but uncached large block read and write speeds drop to 12.0 GB/sec and 10.51 GB/sec respectively. Cryptographic and integer operations are supported by hardware flags such as AES, BMI2, and AVX, though AVX-512 and AMX extensions are absent.

Given its high memory allocation relative to compute resources, this instance offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require intense CPU throughput. It is suitable for small-scale database operations, caching layers like Redis, lightweight web serving, and basic text or image processing tasks. However, it is not recommended for heavy multi-threaded computing, large-scale language model inference, or high-throughput data compilation due to its constrained processing and memory bandwidth limits.
description: Google Cloud Platform e2-highmem-2 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the E2 instance family, configured with 2 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.2 GHz, this instance allocates 8.0 GB of memory per core but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks position this server in the lower tier, with weak single-core and poor multi-core CPU results, alongside restricted memory bandwidth. Qualitatively, the e2-highmem-2 provides a cost-effective option for memory-sensitive workloads that require minimal processing power. It is best suited for lightweight database hosting, Redis caching, static web serving, and simple background data processing tasks where memory capacity is prioritized over raw computational speed.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform e2-highmem-2 is a memory-optimized x86_64 instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, designed for low-compute, memory-sensitive workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform e2-highmem-2 is an x86_64 instance with 2 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, suited for lightweight memory-sensitive workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly, memory-optimized virtual server designed for lightweight caching, small databases, and low-compute background tasks.
bullet_points: 2 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory
Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.2 GHz
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Optimized for lightweight databases and Redis caching
Cost-effective profile for memory-heavy, low-compute workloads
